title: Bollobás–Leader–Tiba Conjecture 1
statement_text: For subsets $A, B$ of $\mathbb{Z}_p$ of size at most $\alpha p$ for an appropriate constant $\alpha > 0$, one only needs three elements $b_1, b_2, b_3 \in B$ to guarantee $|A + \{b_1, b_2, b_3\}| \geq |A| + |B| - 1$.
context_text: Bollobás, Leader and Tiba showed that for subsets $A, B$ of $\mathbb{Z}_p$ with $|A| \geq |B|$, there exists a subset $B^{(C)}$ of $B$ of constant size $C$ such that $|A + B^{(C)}| \geq \min(p, |A| + |B| - 1)$. They conjectured that $C = 3$ suffices when the sets have size at most $\alpha p$. This conjecture is Conjecture 1 of their paper [5].
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full: Theorem 1.3 establishes that for nonempty subsets $A, B$ of $\mathbb{Z}_p$ of size $n \leq \alpha p$ (for some explicit $\alpha > 0$), there exists $B^{(3)} \subseteq B$ of size at most three such that $|A + B^{(3)}| \geq 2n - 1$.
notes: PDF source — some math notation may be garbled. Statement reconstructed from the abstract and Theorem 1.3 statement in the paper body.
paper_title: Small subsets with large sumset: Beyond the Cauchy--Davenport bound

title: Bollobás–Leader–Tiba Question 3 (medium-sized high-dimensional version)
statement_text: For a subset $A \subseteq \mathbb{Z}^d$ that is not contained in the union of any $t$ parallel hyperplanes, does there exist $A' \subseteq A$ with $|A'| \leq C\sqrt{|A|}$ and $|A' + A'| \geq (2d - \varepsilon)|A|$?
context_text: The paper establishes (Theorem 1.4) that a bounded-size subset $A'$ of $A$ suffices to achieve $|A + A'| \geq (2d - \varepsilon)|A|$ when $A$ is not covered by $t$ parallel hyperplanes. Question 3 of [6] asks whether a medium-sized ($O(\sqrt{|A|})$) subset also works for the self-sumset $A' + A'$.
paper_contribution: Answers the question negatively: Proposition 4.1 gives a construction showing that no such medium-sized subset $A'$ can guarantee $|A' + A'| \geq (2d - \varepsilon)|A|$ in general, though the bounded-size version (Theorem 1.4) does hold.
notes: PDF source — math notation may be garbled. Statement reconstructed from the discussion in the introduction surrounding Theorem 1.4 and the reference to [6, Question 3].
paper_title: Small subsets with large sumset: Beyond the Cauchy--Davenport bound

title: Bollobás–Leader–Tiba Question 5 (one-sided saturation)
statement_text: Given constants $\varepsilon > 0$ and $\lambda > 0$, is there a constant $c > 0$ such that the following holds? If $A, B$ are subsets of $\mathbb{Z}_p$ with $|A| = |B| = n$ and $|A + B| \leq \lambda n$, then there exist sets $A' \subseteq A$ and $B' \subseteq B$ such that $|A'| \geq (1 - \varepsilon)|A|$, $|B'| \leq c$, and $|A' + B'| \geq (1 - \varepsilon)|A + B|$.
context_text: Theorem 1.7 (due to Bollobás, Leader and Tiba) shows that one can achieve almost all of $A' + B'$ by passing both $A$ and $B$ to large subsets $A'$ and $B'$ and then selecting a bounded-size $B''$ from $B'$. Question 5 of [5] asks whether it suffices to replace only $A$ (keeping a bounded set from $B$) rather than both sets.
paper_contribution: Answers the question negatively in a strong sense: Theorem 1.8 constructs a set $A \subseteq \mathbb{Z}_p$ of size $\Omega_\nu(p)$ with doubling $|A+A| \leq \lambda|A|$ such that for every $A' \subseteq A$ of size at least $(1-\varepsilon)|A|$ and every $A^{(s)} \subseteq A$ of bounded size $s$, $|A' + A^{(s)}| \leq \nu|A' + A|$. The result also holds over $\mathbb{Z}$ and $\mathbb{F}_2^n$.
notes: PDF source — math notation may be garbled. Statement reconstructed from the explicit paraphrase of Question 5 in the introduction and the discussion preceding Theorem 1.8.
paper_title: Small subsets with large sumset: Beyond the Cauchy--Davenport bound
